item.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Highlights Software Development Pipeline Risk
item.description:
A leaked GitHub token underscores what most organizations get wrong: Treating secrets management as a tooling problem rather than an identity problem.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Stressors, AI Forcing Changes to Cybersecurity Teams
item.description:
As threats proliferate and AI complicates cybersecurity, CISOs say the job is getting harder, but more companies still want cybersecurity expertise, if even on a part-time basis.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: He Thought He Was Secure; His Phone Number Got Stolen Anyway
item.description:
Threat actors can easily steal one-time passwords sent by text when they conduct a SIM swap attack. This can lead to account takeovers, so users must layer up their security measures.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: DifyTap Bugs Let Attackers 'Wiretap' AI Chat Histories
item.description:
Four vulnerabilities allow attackers to exploit Dify, a platform for AI application building and management, to silently access and exfiltrate sensitive data.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
item.description:
The threat actors engineered a Golang-based sniffer to target 430,000 FortiGate firewalls and identify 110 million credentials in the ongoing global campaign.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: SocGholish Takedown Highlights Malicious TDS Threats
item.description:
SocGholish uses traffic distribution systems (TDSs) to provide initial access into victims' networks for cybercrime groups such as the notorious Evil Corp.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 'Cordyceps': Mushrooming Malicious Pull Requests Threaten Developer Workflows
item.description:
The CI/CD workflow weakness affects Microsoft's Azure Sentinel, Google's AI Agent Development Kit, Apache's Doris analytics database, Cloudflare's Workers SDK, and Python Software Foundation's Black.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Scope of Salesforce Attacks Expands as Icarus Leaks Data
item.description:
More victims have emerged after attackers breached application vendor Klue and used its OAuth tokens to steal customers' Salesforce data.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: OpenAI Refocuses Cybersecurity Efforts on Patching Over Discovery
item.description:
OpenAI has expanded its Daybreak cybersecurity initiative with a new suite of tools and partnerships. The post OpenAI Refocuses Cybersecurity Efforts on Patching Over Discovery appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Algerian Man Extradited to US for Running Cybercrime Marketplaces
item.description:
26-year-old Abdellah Belmili faces up to 30 years in prison for allegedly operating the marketplaces Market0Day and Spoxy. The post Algerian Man Extradited to US for Running Cybercrime Marketplaces appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Eight-Year-Old Samsung KNOX Flaw Exposed Millions of Galaxy Devices to Kernel Attacks
item.description:
The high-severity use-after-free vulnerability in Samsung's KNOX security framework affected Android-powered Galaxy devices from the S9 through S25. The post Eight-Year-Old Samsung KNOX Flaw Exposed Millions of Galaxy Devices to Kernel Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Data Exposure Flaws Threaten Dify AI Platform Used by 1 Million Apps
item.description:
Attackers could abuse Dify's multi-tenant cloud service to read private chats, preview other tenants' documents, and reach internal APIs. The post Data Exposure Flaws Threaten Dify AI Platform Used by 1 Million Apps appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Dragos Unveils AI for OT Security
item.description:
Named EmberAI, the new capability is built on Dragos’ massive operational technology cybersecurity dataset. The post Dragos Unveils AI for OT Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hackers Exploiting Cisco Unified CM Vulnerability
item.description:
Cisco noted that a PoC had been available for CVE-2026-20230 when it announced patches in early June. The post Hackers Exploiting Cisco Unified CM Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Two Scattered Spider members plead guilty over cyberattack that crippled London transit
item.description:
A 20-year-old and an 18-year-old admitted to infiltrating the network of Transport for London in 2024, disrupting public transportation services for months.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Compromise kids online safety bill unveiled by House leaders, with key omission
item.description:
The so-called duty of care provision that was excluded would have mandated that online platforms take reasonable measures to prevent specific harms such as suicidal ideation, eating disorders and cyberbullying by changing algorithm and design features.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Trump directs federal agencies to protect US data from quantum threats
item.description:
An executive order signed Monday aims to accelerate the government's transition to post-quantum cryptography (PQC), a new generation of encryption designed to protect data from the powerful quantum computers expected in the future.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Feds seize alleged cyber-scam infrastructure connected to Southeast Asian company
item.description:
The Department of Justice announced the “seizure of a cloud computing account” used by subsidiaries of the Huione Group, a conglomerate severed from the U.S. financial system last year.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Five Eyes agencies sound alarm about AI’s threat to cybersecurity
item.description:
"The timeline is not years, it is months,” the nations of the Five Eyes intelligence alliance said in a joint alert about the cybersecurity concerns of artificial intelligence.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
item.description:
Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
item.description:
Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
item.description:
Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
item.description:
The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
item.description:
A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
item.description:
Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Multi-million-dollar Settlement Agreed to Resolve MCNA Dental Data Breach Lawsuit
item.description:
A settlement has been agreed to resolve class action data breach litigation against Managed Care of North America (MCNA), Inc., […] The post Multi-million-dollar Settlement Agreed to Resolve MCNA Dental Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: ShinyHunters Data Extortion Group Threatens to Leak 8.8 TB of Stolen One Medical Data
item.description:
One Medical, the Amazon-owned primary care provider, has recently announced a cybersecurity incident in which an unauthorized third party gained […] The post ShinyHunters Data Extortion Group Threatens to Leak 8.8 TB of Stolen One Medical Data appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Blue Fish Pediatrics Data Breach Affects More Than 41,000 Texas Patients
item.description:
Blue Fish Pediatrics in Texas has announced a July 2025 cyberattack that affected more than 41,000 Texas patients. Data breaches […] The post Blue Fish Pediatrics Data Breach Affects More Than 41,000 Texas Patients appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Xsolis Data Breach Affects 1.4M Individuals
item.description:
Xsolis, a business associate of HIPAA-covered entities that provides AI-powered solutions for improving case and utilization management to achieve more […] The post Xsolis Data Breach Affects 1.4M Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: LifePoint Health; Southwest Behavioral & Health Services; Nottingham Village Report Data Breaches
item.description:
Data breaches have been announced by Lifepoint Health, Southwest Behavioral & Health Services, and Nottingham Village. Lifepoint Health Lifepoint Health […] The post LifePoint Health; Southwest Behavioral & Health Services; Nottingham Village Report Data Breaches appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Free Webinar: AI + HIPAA: Innovating in Healthcare Without Leaving Compliance Behind
item.description:
Artificial intelligence has tremendous potential in healthcare, and healthcare organizations have embraced AI tools in all areas of their operation; […] The post Free Webinar: AI + HIPAA: Innovating in Healthcare Without Leaving Compliance Behind appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: April 2026 Healthcare Data Breach Report
item.description:
In April 2026, 47 healthcare data breaches affecting 500 or more individuals were reported to the HHS’ Office for Civil […] The post April 2026 Healthcare Data Breach Report appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Unknown
item.description:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: JaredFromSubway MEV bot hacked in $15 million crypto theft
item.description:
The JaredFromSubway Ethereum MEV (Maximal Extractable Value) bot suffered a $15 million loss after an attacker manipulated the opportunity-detection logic by creating fake cryptocurrency trading opportunities. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: WhatsApp phishing attack uses fake business docs to hack PCs
item.description:
An ongoing malware campaign is targeting WhatsApp users in multiple countries with deceptive messages that push VBScript files, leading to remote system access. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: LastPass confirms data breach in Klue supply chain attack
item.description:
LastPass announced that hackers accessed customer data from its Salesforce environment after stealing the company's OAuth tokens in the Klue supply chain attack earlier this month. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Scattered Spider members plead guilty to hacking Transport for London
item.description:
Two members of the 'Scattered Spider' cybercrime group pleaded guilty to hacking the Transport for London (TfL) systems in 2024. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: New macOS ClickFix attack silently mounts DMGs to push infostealer
item.description:
A new macOS ClickFix campaign is using Terminal commands to silently download, mount, and launch info-stealing malware from malicious disk image (DMG) files. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Healthtech firm Xolis suffers data breach impacting 1.4 million people
item.description:
Healthcare technology company Xsolis says that sensitive data belonging to nearly 1.4 million individuals was compromised in a phishing attack that gave attackers access to its network. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Tata Electronics confirms cyberattack as hackers leak data
item.description:
Tata Electronics has confirmed in a statement to BleepingComputer that it was the target of a cyberattack that impacted parts of its IT infrastructure. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Cisco Unified CM flaw CVE-2026-20230 now exploited in attacks
item.description:
A high-severity SSRF vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20230, in Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server is now being exploited in attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: GMS Report Victim gms.net Threat Actor Icarus Date Discovered Jun 23, 2026 Description GMS (formerly Global Message Services) is a communications technology company based â¦June 23, 2026
item.description:

item.title: Bombay High Court Blocks FulcrumSec Data Leak (3)
item.description:
Another day, another injunction. When DataBreaches read the news headline, our first thought was that this was an injunction sought by Global Schools Group. Our first impression was correct, but it took a reminder from FulcrumSec to realize that it was GSG-connected. Ananya Iyer reports: The Bombay High Court issued an interim order restraining the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Global Schools Group Obtained Two Court Injunctions That Didn’t Seem to Change Much—and Might Backfire (1)
item.description:
Following a major data security incident involving sensitive student and parent information, Global Schools Group sought court injunctions prohibiting the publication of data acquired by FulcrumSec. They obtained the injunctions, but once again, injunctions do not affect threat actors — or at least, not in the way the plaintiffs hoped. Yesterday, DataBreaches reported that Global... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Klue OAuth breach victim list grows as Icarus hackers claim attack
item.description:
Lawrence Abrams reports: Market intelligence platform Klue has publicly confirmed a recent security incident that allowed threat actors to steal OAuth tokens used to connect to customers’ Salesforce environments, as the new “Icarus” extortion group publicly claims the attack. The disclosure comes after cybersecurity firms Huntress and ReliaQuest detailed how attackers abused compromised Klue Battlecards integrations to steal Salesforce... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Two Data Breaches Didn’t Sink Novo Nordisk’s Stock. Why Not?
item.description:
June was a challenging month for Novo Nordisk regarding cybersecurity and intellectual property protection. The pharma giant allegedly had some of its data — including intellectual property — stolen by two independent groups of threat actors. Unaware of each other, each group claimed to have acquired a large amount of valuable information. One demanded $25... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Brazil’s Civil Defense suffers a cyberattack on its official alert network
item.description:
This is the kind of cyberattack that can put lives at risk and makes me want to wring some necks if I wasn’t so old and feeble. Demócrata reports: Brazil’s Civil Defense has reported this Saturday that its official alert system has been the target of a cyberattack, an incident that is already being investigated... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Cherry Health provides preliminary notice of recent data breach
item.description:
On April 19, 2026, Cherry Health in Michigan detected suspicious network activity. Investigation revealed that an unknown person or persons had gained access to its network and copied data. On June 18, Cherry Health published a preliminary notice on its website. The notice makes no mention of any earlier reporting on the incident that had... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Xsolis breach affected 1,396,519 of its clients’ patients
item.description:
Xsolis, Inc. is a business associate in the healthcare sector, providing utilization and case management services. They describe themselves as applying “industry-leading AI and automation to ensure appropriate care settings and accelerate collaboration across a connected network of providers and payers.” On June 19, California Attorney General’s Office posted a copy of a breach notification... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Two men, believed to part of Scattered Spiders, plead guilty over £39m TfL cyber attack
item.description:
Two members of Scattered Spider, who were arrested in 2024 and 2025, have reportedly changed their pleas to guilty just before their trials were set to begin. Victoria Collins reports: Two men have pleaded guilty to offences in connection with a massive cyber attack which caused Transport for London (TfL) months of disruption and cost... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Cybersecurity Incidents: The Problem Isn’t Just Who Attacks
item.description:
Over on SuspectFile, Marco A. De Felice reflects on how we may overfocus on identifying threat actors exploiting vulnerabilities while failing to focus enough on root causes and incident response. He highlights what he calls a structural fragility that cannot be ignored: that many organizations continue to collect, centralize, and retain vast amounts of sensitive... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: “The Timeline Is Months, Not Years”: Five Eyes Warns of AI-Powered Cyberattacks
item.description:
MITSloan reports: The intelligence alliance of the United States, United Kingdom, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand, commonly known as Five Eyes, has raised concerns over rapidly advancing artificial intelligence, which can supercharge offensive hacking capabilities. In a three-page statement, the alliance called for urgent action to confront the threat. “Frontier AI models are anticipated to... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.description:
